news_app package¶

Submodules¶

news_app.admin module¶

class news_app.admin.UserAdmin(model, admin_site)[source]¶

Bases: UserAdmin

Custom admin for User model to display role and publisher information.

fieldsets = ((None, {'fields': ('username', 'password')}), ('Personal info', {'fields': ('first_name', 'last_name', 'email')}), ('Permissions', {'fields': ('is_active', 'is_staff', 'is_superuser', 'groups', 'user_permissions')}), ('Important dates', {'fields': ('last_login', 'date_joined')}), ('Role & Publisher', {'fields': ('role', 'publisher', 'subscribed_publishers', 'subscribed_journalists')}))¶
property media¶
news_app.admin.setup_groups(modeladmin, request, queryset)[source]¶

Admin action to manually trigger group and permission creation.

news_app.api_views module¶

class news_app.api_views.ApprovedArticleLogView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: CreateAPIView

API endpoint to log approved articles. Simulates external sharing by receiving article details via POST.

create(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]¶
permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>]¶
serializer_class¶

alias of ArticleSerializer

class news_app.api_views.ArticleDetailAPIView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: RetrieveUpdateDestroyAPIView

GET /api/articles/<id>/ - Retrieve an article. PUT /api/articles/<id>/ - Update article (editors/journalists). DELETE /api/articles/<id>/ - Delete article (editors/journalists).

get_permissions()[source]¶

Instantiates and returns the list of permissions that this view requires.

perform_update(serializer)[source]¶
permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>]¶
queryset = <QuerySet [<Article: The Future of AI in Software Engineering (By: Jane Publisher)>, <Article: Understanding Microservices Architecture (By: Jane Publisher)>, <Article: Best Practices for CI/CD Pipelines (By: Jane Publisher)>, <Article: Demystifying Rust for Systems Programming (By: Jane Publisher)>, <Article: Best AI For Studying - YouLearn.ai (By: Jane Journalist)>]>¶
serializer_class¶

alias of ArticleSerializer

class news_app.api_views.ArticleListCreateAPIView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ListAPIView, CreateAPIView

GET /api/articles/ - Return all approved articles. POST /api/articles/ - Create article (journalists only).

get_permissions()[source]¶

Instantiates and returns the list of permissions that this view requires.

get_queryset()[source]¶

Get the list of items for this view. This must be an iterable, and may be a queryset. Defaults to using self.queryset.

This method should always be used rather than accessing self.queryset directly, as self.queryset gets evaluated only once, and those results are cached for all subsequent requests.

You may want to override this if you need to provide different querysets depending on the incoming request.

(Eg. return a list of items that is specific to the user)

perform_create(serializer)[source]¶
permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>]¶
serializer_class¶

alias of ArticleSerializer

class news_app.api_views.IsEditorOrAuthor[source]¶

Bases: BasePermission

Permission class allowing only Editors or the original Author to update/delete.

has_object_permission(request, view, obj)[source]¶

Return True if permission is granted, False otherwise.

class news_app.api_views.IsJournalist[source]¶

Bases: BasePermission

Permission class allowing only Journalists to perform write operations (POST).

has_permission(request, view)[source]¶

Return True if permission is granted, False otherwise.

class news_app.api_views.SubscribedArticlesAPIView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ListAPIView

GET /api/articles/subscribed/ - Return articles only from followed publishers/journalists.

get_queryset()[source]¶

Get the list of items for this view. This must be an iterable, and may be a queryset. Defaults to using self.queryset.

This method should always be used rather than accessing self.queryset directly, as self.queryset gets evaluated only once, and those results are cached for all subsequent requests.

You may want to override this if you need to provide different querysets depending on the incoming request.

(Eg. return a list of items that is specific to the user)

permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>]¶
serializer_class¶

alias of ArticleSerializer

Bases: AppConfig

Application configuration for the news app.

name = 'news_app'¶
ready()[source]¶

Register signal handlers when the app is loaded.

news_app.models module¶

class news_app.models.Article(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: Model

Represents a news article with a workflow: Draft -> Pending -> Approved/Rejected.

APPROVED = 'approved'¶
DRAFT = 'draft'¶
exception DoesNotExist¶

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned¶

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

exception NotUpdated¶

Bases: ObjectNotUpdated, DatabaseError

PENDING = 'pending'¶
POSTER_CHOICES = [('journalist', 'Independent Journalist'), ('publisher', 'Publisher-Branded')]¶
POSTER_JOURNALIST = 'journalist'¶
POSTER_PUBLISHER = 'publisher'¶
REJECTED = 'rejected'¶
STATUS_CHOICES = [('draft', 'Draft'), ('pending', 'Pending Review'), ('approved', 'Approved'), ('rejected', 'Rejected')]¶
approved¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

approved_by¶

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

author¶

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

get_status_display(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.CharField: status>)¶
id¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

image¶

Just like the FileDescriptor, but for ImageFields. The only difference is assigning the width/height to the width_field/height_field, if appropriate.

newsletters¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

objects = <django.db.models.manager.Manager object>¶
poster_type¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

class news_app.models.Newsletter(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: Model

A collection of articles curated for a publisher’s subscribers. Integrates many-to-many relationship with approved publisher articles.

class news_app.models.User(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: AbstractUser

Custom user model supporting roles: Reader, Editor, and Journalist. Enforces unique email addresses.

exception DoesNotExist¶

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

EDITOR = 'editor'¶
JOURNALIST = 'journalist'¶
exception MultipleObjectsReturned¶

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

exception NotUpdated¶

Bases: ObjectNotUpdated, DatabaseError

READER = 'reader'¶
ROLE_CHOICES = [('reader', 'Reader'), ('editor', 'Editor'), ('journalist', 'Journalist')]¶
approved_articles¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_reverse_many_to_one_manager() defined below.

auth_token¶

Accessor to the related object on the reverse side of a one-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Restaurant(Model):
    place = OneToOneField(Place, related_name='restaurant')

Place.restaurant is a ReverseOneToOneDescriptor instance.

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

news_app.signals module¶

news_app.signals.assign_user_to_group(sender, instance, created, **kwargs)[source]¶

Keep Django auth groups synchronized with the user’s selected role.

news_app.signals.create_groups_and_permissions()[source]¶

Create role groups and attach the expected article permissions.

news_app.signals.ensure_groups_after_migrate(sender, **kwargs)[source]¶

Automatically create/update role groups and permissions after migrations.

news_app.signals.notify_subscribers_on_approval(sender, instance, **kwargs)[source]¶

Notify subscribed readers when an article is approved.

news_app.tests module¶

class news_app.tests.ArticleApprovalWorkflowTest(methodName='runTest')[source]¶

Bases: TestCase

setUp()[source]¶

Hook method for setting up the test fixture before exercising it.

test_non_editor_cannot_approve_article()[source]¶
class news_app.tests.JournalistAndEditorCRUDActionsTest(methodName='runTest')[source]¶

Bases: TestCase

Test suite for new Journalist/Editor CRUD actions on articles and newsletters.

setUp()[source]¶

Hook method for setting up the test fixture before exercising it.

test_editor_can_create_newsletter()[source]¶

test_editor_cannot_update_newsletter_different_publisher()[source]¶

test_journalist_cannot_update_other_newsletter()[source]¶
class news_app.tests.RESTAPIComprehensiveTest(methodName='runTest')[source]¶

Bases: TestCase

Rubric-focused automated tests verifying roles, subscriptions, auth token, and CRUD restrictions on the new REST API.

setUp()[source]¶

Hook method for setting up the test fixture before exercising it.

test_editor_can_delete_article_via_api()[source]¶
test_global_approved_articles_list()[source]¶
test_journalist_can_create_article_via_api()[source]¶
test_post_approval_subscriber_email_trigger(mock_send_mail)[source]¶
test_reader_cannot_create_article_via_api()[source]¶
test_subscribed_articles_feed()[source]¶
test_token_retrieval_endpoint()[source]¶
class news_app.tests.RoleLoginRedirectTest(methodName='runTest')[source]¶

Bases: TestCase

Ensure users land on the correct dashboard after login.

setUp()[source]¶

Hook method for setting up the test fixture before exercising it.

test_editor_redirected_to_editor_dashboard()[source]¶
test_journalist_redirected_to_journalist_dashboard()[source]¶
test_reader_redirected_to_reader_dashboard()[source]¶
class news_app.tests.SignupViewTest(methodName='runTest')[source]¶

Bases: TestCase

Validate account registration behavior from the public signup page.

test_signup_creates_editor_when_role_selected()[source]¶
test_signup_creates_journalist_when_role_selected()[source]¶
test_signup_creates_reader_and_redirects_to_login()[source]¶
test_signup_page_is_accessible()[source]¶
test_signup_rejects_duplicate_username()[source]¶
test_signup_rejects_mismatched_passwords()[source]¶
test_signup_requires_email()[source]¶
test_signup_with_invalid_role_falls_back_to_reader()[source]¶
class news_app.tests.SubscribedArticlesAPITest(methodName='runTest')[source]¶

Bases: TestCase

setUp()[source]¶

Hook method for setting up the test fixture before exercising it.

test_draft_articles_not_returned_in_api()[source]¶
test_reader_subscribed_to_journalist_sees_journalist_article()[source]¶
test_reader_subscribed_to_publisher_sees_publisher_article()[source]¶
test_unauthenticated_user_denied()[source]¶
test_unsubscribed_reader_sees_no_articles()[source]¶
class news_app.tests.UserGroupAssignmentTest(methodName='runTest')[source]¶

Bases: TestCase

setUp()[source]¶

Hook method for setting up the test fixture before exercising it.

test_editor_assigned_to_editor_group()[source]¶
test_journalist_assigned_to_journalist_group()[source]¶
test_reader_assigned_to_reader_group()[source]¶

news_app.urls module¶

URL routing configuration for the news_app application. Maps views to endpoints for Reader, Editor, and Journalist actions, as well as subscription operations and REST API endpoints.

news_app.views module¶

news_app.views.approve_article(request, pk)[source]¶

Approve or reject a pending article. Editor-only.

news_app.views.article_detail(request, pk)[source]¶

Show a single approved article to authenticated users. Includes suggested articles (latest 3 approved excluding current).

news_app.views.change_status(request, pk)[source]¶

Quickly change the status of an article.

news_app.views.delete_article(request, pk)[source]¶

Allow editors and journalists (authors) to delete articles.

news_app.views.edit_profile(request)[source]¶

Allow journalists (and editors) to edit their profile picture and bio.

news_app.views.editor_dashboard(request)[source]¶

List pending articles for editors to review, and provide access to article management.

news_app.views.ensure_demo_accounts()[source]¶

Ensure role demo accounts exist with known credentials for evaluation.

news_app.views.home(request)[source]¶

Render the public homepage with only approved articles.

news_app.views.journalist_dashboard(request)[source]¶

Display journalist-owned articles and newsletters.

news_app.views.journalist_profile(request, pk)[source]¶

Public platform for a journalist showing their articles and trust metrics.

news_app.views.login_view(request)[source]¶

Authenticate a user with username/password and start a session.

news_app.views.logout_view(request)[source]¶

End the current session and send the user to the login page.

news_app.views.newsletter_create(request)[source]¶

Allow journalists and editors to create newsletters and select articles.

news_app.views.newsletter_delete(request, pk)[source]¶

Allow journalists (authors) and editors to delete newsletters.

news_app.views.newsletter_detail(request, pk)[source]¶

Display the details of a specific newsletter.

news_app.views.newsletter_list(request)[source]¶

Display a list of all newsletters. Accessible to all logged-in users.

news_app.views.newsletter_update(request, pk)[source]¶

Allow journalists (authors) and editors to update newsletters. Restricts access so that editors can only edit within their own publisher.

news_app.views.publisher_create(request)[source]¶

Allow Editors to create a new publisher.

news_app.views.publisher_edit(request, pk)[source]¶

Allow editors to edit their publisher’s details.

news_app.views.publisher_list(request)[source]¶

Display a list of all publishers. Accessible to Editors.

news_app.views.publisher_profile(request, pk)[source]¶

Public platform for a publisher showing their articles and trust metrics.

news_app.views.reader_dashboard(request)[source]¶

Display user-specific content for readers, including followed publishers/journalists.

news_app.views.setup_permissions_view(request)[source]¶

Create default role groups/permissions. Superuser-only utility endpoint.

news_app.views.signup_view(request)[source]¶

Create a new user account with a specific role. Validates that the username and email are unique.

news_app.views.submit_article(request)[source]¶

Allow journalists to submit new articles for editor review. Allows selection of poster type (Independent vs Publisher).

news_app.views.subscribe_to_journalist(request, pk)[source]¶

Subscribe the current reader to a journalist.

news_app.views.subscribe_to_publisher(request, pk)[source]¶

Subscribe the current reader to a publisher.

news_app.views.unsubscribe_from_journalist(request, pk)[source]¶

Unsubscribe the current reader from a journalist.

news_app.views.unsubscribe_from_publisher(request, pk)[source]¶

Unsubscribe the current reader from a publisher.

news_app.views.update_article(request, pk)[source]¶

Allow editors and journalists (authors) to update articles.

Module contents¶

Main application package news_app for publishing and subscription workflows.

News System
